cons: Potential dental erosion due to acidity

ingredients_analysis.name: Carbonated Water
ingredients_analysis.reason: Hydrating and generally harmless.

ingredients_analysis.name: Caramel E150d
ingredients_analysis.reason: May contain potentially harmful compounds formed during manufacturing.

ingredients_analysis.name: Aspartame
ingredients_analysis.reason: An artificial sweetener with potential health concerns for some individuals.

ingredients_analysis.name: Acesulfame K
ingredients_analysis.reason: Another artificial sweetener with limited long-term safety data.

ingredients_analysis.name: Phosphoric Acid
ingredients_analysis.reason: Can interfere with calcium absorption and contribute to dental erosion.

ingredients_analysis.name: Citric Acid
ingredients_analysis.reason: Can contribute to dental erosion with frequent consumption.

ingredients_analysis.name: Flavourings (including caffeine)
ingredients_analysis.reason: Unspecified flavorings can hide potentially problematic additives; caffeine can cause jitters and anxiety.

ingredients_analysis.name: Potassium Sorbate
ingredients_analysis.reason: A common preservative, generally considered safe in small amounts.

sandeep.marketing_vs_reality: The 'source of phenylalanine' claim is factual for those with phenylketonuria (PKU), but it does little to highlight any benefits for the general population and may even mislead consumers into thinking it has some positive attribute.
sandeep.community_takeaway: Choose water, unsweetened tea, or homemade lemonade to quench your thirst instead of this soda.
